title: q-counting of column-height-exactly-m partitions = truncated q-negative-binomial RHS

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Granting the basis lemma, prove (S2->S3):

sum_{ lambda in (l^k), lambda'_1 = m } q^{|lambda|} = q^m * qbinom(l,m) * sum_{j=0}^{k-m} q^{ j(l-m+1) } * qbinom(m+j-1, j).

Suggested route: transpose to mu = lambda' in (k^l) box with largest part mu_1 = m (<= l parts, each <= k). Split off the maximal-part columns and the sub-diagram of parts < m. Tools:
- q-Pascal: qbinom(n,k)=qbinom(n-1,k-1)+q^k qbinom(n-1,k) (and dual).
- q-negative-binomial identity: sum_{j>=0} qbinom(m+j-1,j) z^j = prod_{i=0}^{m-1}(1-z q^i)^{-1}, specialized z = q^{l-m+1} and TRUNCATED at j=k-m by box height k.

Pitfalls (G3): the prefactor q^m * qbinom(l,m) (not merely qbinom(l,m)), the exponent shift l-m+1, and the truncation j<=k-m must each be derived, not pattern-matched. Provide an explicit weight-preserving bijection or a fully justified q-binomial manipulation.
